title: 中考六选五 16: The Problem with “Almost Understanding”
passage:
Many students have had this experience: when the teacher explains something, they feel they understand it. But when they try to do the exercise alone, they suddenly get stuck. 1. ______ This is called “almost understanding”.

Almost understanding is dangerous because it feels like real understanding. Students may nod in class and believe they have learned the point. 2. ______ Without practice, they may not notice the missing steps in their thinking.

One common reason is that watching is easier than doing. When the teacher solves a problem, each step may look clear. 3. ______ The student does not have to decide what to do next, so the difficulty is hidden.

To turn almost understanding into real understanding, students need active practice. They should close the book and try to explain the idea in their own words. 4. ______ If they cannot do these things, they probably need more practice.

Mistakes are useful in this process. They show exactly where understanding breaks down. 5. ______ In this way, confusion becomes a guide instead of a reason to give up.

questions.label: Questions 1-5 - Sentence Insertion
questions.instruction: Choose the best sentence from A-F for each blank in the passage. There is one extra sentence you do not need.

choices.text: But the real test comes when they must choose the steps by themselves.
They should also try similar questions without looking at the examples.
This means they understand part of the idea, but not enough to use it independently.
However, this confidence may disappear as soon as they work alone.
Students should avoid difficult exercises until they feel completely ready.
Finding and correcting these weak points can make learning more complete.
